palette_hint: Deep navy background #0B1220 with off-white text #F0EDE6, accented by molten amber #E8A020 and electric teal #1ECFC4
type_style: Georgia for headings (bold 700, italic on hero), system-ui for body at 400/500 weight — refined, editorial feel with generous letter-spacing on caps
description: Lumina Glass Studio is a boutique architectural glass art company marketing custom commission panels, decorative installations, and a signature blown-glass home collection to interior designers and high-end residential clients.
constraints: Max content width 1200px centered, with full-bleed gradient sections behind it; section vertical padding is always 80px top and bottom
Elevation system: cards rest at box-shadow 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.4), hover lifts to 0 8px 32px rgba(232,160,32,0.35), hero elements use 0 16px 64px rgba(0,0,0,0.7)
Border-radius rhythm: buttons use 999px pill, cards use 12px, images use 4px, section dividers use 0; never mix outside this set
Every major section must include at least one ::before or ::after pseudo-element used as a decorative accent — either a radial gradient orb, an amber horizontal rule, or a teal geometric slash
Glass-effect cards (backdrop-filter: blur(12px), background rgba(255,255,255,0.06), border 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12)) are used on the process steps and contact form panels

page_specs.showcase: Full-viewport hero with a radial gradient orb in amber centered behind the headline 'Light Made Permanent', a 14px all-caps teal subheading 'Architectural Glass Art Since 1997', and a pill CTA button in amber. Below the hero, a three-column asymmetric grid (one wide, two narrow) displays featured installations — 'The Meridian Atrium', 'Cascade Residence Panels', and 'Hotel Solène Chandelier' — each as a 12px-radius card with amber-glow hover shadow and a short two-line description in off-white.
page_specs.process: A numbered four-step horizontal timeline section on a deep-navy-to-midnight-blue linear gradient background, each step rendered as a glass-effect card (blur + translucent border) with a large amber step number (80px, Georgia italic), a bold heading like 'Concept & Sketch', and two sentences of concrete process detail. A decorative teal diagonal slash pseudo-element sits behind the step numbers. A pull-quote in Georgia italic 18px — 'Every commission begins with listening' — sits centered between the timeline and a secondary CTA row.
page_specs.collections: A product showcase page for three named collections: 'Solstice Series' (amber and gold blown vessels), 'Arctic Shelf' (clear and ice-blue panel work), and 'Ember Column' (freestanding sculptural glass). Each collection occupies a full-width alternating two-column row — image placeholder block on one side, rich descriptive copy and a pill ghost-button on the other — with a 4px-radius image block, 12px-radius text container, and an amber linear gradient underline on each collection name heading.
page_specs.studio: An about-the-studio page opening with a two-column layout: a 400px-wide text block introducing founder 'Mara Holvenstot' and her 26-year practice, alongside a stacked pair of image placeholder blocks with drop-shadow filter. Below, three glass-effect stat cards display '340+ Commissions', '18 Countries', and '6 Industry Awards' in large amber numerals. A closing full-bleed banner with a radial gradient background and italic Georgia quote — 'Glass remembers heat long after the flame is gone' — features a centered amber decorative rule 60px wide as a ::after element.
page_specs.contact: A commission inquiry page with a centered glass-effect form panel (max-width 640px, 12px radius, blur backdrop, amber border on focus fields via outline) containing fields for Name, Studio or Firm, Project Type (dropdown: Residential / Commercial / Public Art), Budget Range, and Message, plus a pill submit button in amber. Above the form, a two-column header row lists studio address '14 Foundry Lane, Portland OR' and email 'commissions@luminaglass.studio' in teal, each preceded by a small amber square pseudo-element bullet. Page background uses a subtle linear gradient from #0B1220 to #101828.
